title: Controlled Vocabulary and Ontologies
text:
Code
import requests
import pandas as pd

CT = "https://clinicaltables.nlm.nih.gov/api/"
FHIR_URL = "https://snowstorm-training.snomedtools.org/fhir/ValueSet/$expand"

title: Controlled Vocabulary and Ontologies
text:
Controlled Vocabulary
Below you will explore some of the commonly used controlled vocabularies in biomedical data: ICD-10 for diseases, LOINC for procedures, …
For each of these vocabularies there are also different dedicated DB.
Here I use NLM Clinical Tables because it provides a simple unified interface: NLM Clinical Tables.

Diseases (ICD-10)

ICD history (WHO)
ICD-10 @ clinicaltables documentation



Code
url = CT + "icd10cm/v3/search"

term = "vitamin D deficiency"
# search for {term} in search fields sf: "code" and "name"
params = {"sf": "code,name", "terms": term, "maxList": 100}

r = requests.get(url, params=params)
r.raise_for_status()
print(f"Request Status: {r.status_code}.")

data = r.json()
n = data[0]                # number of matches
df = pd.DataFrame(data[3]) # convert list to df

print(f"{n} matches for term: {term}:")
df.head()


Request Status: 200.
24 matches for term: vitamin D deficiency:









0
1

Vitamin D deficiency, unspecified

D51.0
Vitamin B12 deficiency anemia due to intrinsic...


2
D51.1
Vitamin B12 deficiency anemia due to selective...


3
D51.3
Other dietary vitamin B12 deficiency anemia


4
D51.8
Other vitamin B12 deficiency anemias









Procedures (LOINC)

LOINC @ clinicaltables documentation



Code
url = CT + "loinc_items/v3/search"

term = "blood glucose test"
params = {
    "terms": term,
    "df": "LOINC_NUM,LONG_COMMON_NAME,SHORTNAME",
    "maxList": 200
}

data = requests.get(url, params=params).json()
pd.DataFrame(data[3]).head()

ImportantCV Tasks




With ICD-10:

search for “sickle cell”, “influenza”, “type 2 diabetes”, or a disease of your choice
How many matched ICD-10 codes does this contain?

With LOINC:

How many different “chest Xray” are recorded in LOINC?
Which gene is tested for “Glu6Val” mutation?

title: Controlled Vocabulary and Ontologies
text:
Ontologies
Large-scale effort to represent medical knowledge.

Snomed Browser
Snowstorm-Training

Here you will explore programmatic requests to Snowmed CT and ontology relationships.

FHIR queries

Search for ConceptID
Example: Identify the Snomed ID for the disorder concept “diabetes mellitus”


Code
concept = "diabetes mellitus"
params = {
    "url": "http://snomed.info/sct?fhir_vs",
    "filter": concept,
    "count": 20
}

r = requests.get(FHIR_URL, params=params)
r.raise_for_status()
results = r.json()["expansion"]["contains"]
pd.DataFrame(results).head()









system
code
display




0
http://snomed.info/sct
73211009
DM - Diabetes mellitus


1
http://snomed.info/sct
160303001
Family history of diabetes mellitus


2
http://snomed.info/sct
161445009
H/O: diabetes mellitus


3
http://snomed.info/sct
46635009
Diabetes mellitus type I


4
http://snomed.info/sct
44054006
Type II diabetes mellitus









Query ECL Template
Snomed CT codes form a large ontology. Queries into this data and be formulated with a so called Expression Constraint Language (ECL).


Code
def query_ecl(ecl_expression):
    """
    Sends ECL query to FHIR endpoint
    Returns list of concepts names     
    """
    params = {
        "url": f"http://snomed.info/sct?fhir_vs=ecl/{ecl_expression}",
        "count": 500
    }
    r = requests.get(FHIR_URL, params=params)
    r.raise_for_status()
    
    data = r.json() 
    # data.keys(): resourceType, url (=query), expansion

    # return list of concepts data["expansion"]["contains"]
    return data.get("expansion", {}).get("contains", [])


Simple Usage Example: retrieve a single concept


Code
disease = "73211009" # Diabetes mellitus

query = f"{disease}" # very basic ECL query
results = query_ecl(query)

pd.DataFrame(results)









system
code
display




0
http://snomed.info/sct
73211009
DM - Diabetes mellitus









Complex ECL queries
More complicated ECL queries can also be used to search and retrieve multiple (related) concepts and attributes from the Snomed Ontology, e.g.

concept: “{code}”
parents: “>{code}”
children: “<{code}”
more examples: ECL quick reference







ImportantOntology Task




Find the Snomed CT code for disease “Obesity”
Find the ECL expression to determine

all parents concept for disease “obesity”
all children diseases for disease “obesity”

Determine all attributes (“Due to”) for disease “Diabetes Mellitus”
Help

ECL help
Snowstorm Training. Browser & API




Code
disease="..."                # find code for "obesity"

# find ECL expression for 
parents  = query_ecl(f"...") # children (=direct descendants)
children = query_ecl(f"...") # parents (=direct ancestors)


print(f'Retrieved: {disease}')
print('Parents:  \n', pd.DataFrame(parents))
print('Children: \n', pd.DataFrame(children))

disease = "..."              # find code for Diabetes mellitus
attrib  = "..."              # find code for attribute "due to"
results = query_ecl(f"...")  # find query to extract attributes
print('Attributes: \n', pd.DataFrame(results))
